Q1.text: Norfolk Southern Combined Railroad Subsidiaries* (NS Rail) is comprised principally of Norfolk Southern Railway Consolidated.

Q2.text: Norfolk Southern Railway Company was incorporated June 18, 1894, under the name Southern Railway Company.

text_list: Norfolk Southern Railway Company - Organized under and by virtue of an act of Assembly of the State of Virginia, approved February 20, 1894.

Q4.text: On June 1, 1982, Southern Railway Company (SR) and Norfolk and Western Railway Company (NW) became subsidiaries of Norfolk Southern Corporation (NS), a transportation holding company incorporated in Virginia. Effective December 31, 1990, NS transferred all the common stock of NW to SR, and SR's name was changed to Norfolk Southern Railway Company (NSR). Effective September 1, 1998, NW was merged with and into NSR. In August 1998, the STB's decision approving the joint application of NS, NSR and other parties to control Conrail (which owns Consolidated Rail Corporation) became final. NSR and CSX Transportation began operating their respective portions of Conrail's routes and assets on June 1, 1999. See also note 13 to Schedule 200 on page 14.

B.notes_and_remarks: * See note on page 4 "Principles of Combined Reporting." Q5: Not applicable for "Norfolk Southern Combined Railroad Subsidiaries." Enclosed with this Report Form R-1 are copies of Annual Reports on Form 10-K for year ended December 31, 2008, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission by Norfolk Southern Corporation and Norfolk Southern Railway Company.
